sanskrit:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।
स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वान्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्॥
transliteration:
śreyān sva-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣṭhitāt
svabhāva-niyataṃ karma kurvān nāpnoti kilbiṣam
literal_meaning: Better is one’s own duty, though imperfect, than another’s duty well-performed; doing work ordained by one’s own nature, one incurs no sin.
contextual_meaning: Authentic action aligned with innate temperament—however flawed—protects self-esteem and prevents moral injury; mimicking alien roles invites chronic guilt and identity diffusion.
psychological_state: Post-decisional clarity: anxiety about role-performance drops when inner values and outer action synchronize; perfectionism yields to 'good-enough' authenticity.
trigger_scenario: A software engineer from a family of doctors feels fraudulent in medical school; returning to coding—even with average grades—feels morally cleaner than topping the anatomy class while betraying her true aptitude.
anchor_text: Follow your own path imperfectly rather than another's perfectly.
